title: Sony clarifies its policies on physical discs and inactive accounts on PlayStation
summary: The company assures that it will continue reprinting disc games and that account cancellation due to inactivity is not new, while its disc factory is repurposed for microlenses.

title: Artificial intelligence marks the news with advances in coding, education, and debate on proprietary models
summary: Claude Code is used to port games and is banned by Alibaba, new AI tutors appear, thefts in data centers are reported, and Mistral executives warn about corporate privacy.

title: The retro video game world offers curiosities like Half-Life 2 in the browser and the PS1 inside a DualShock
summary: From the Steam controller prototype to a mod that puts a PS1 console inside a controller, including the possibility of playing Half-Life 2 in the browser at 100 FPS and the Steam sale simulator.

title: Cybersecurity threats increase with agentive ransomware, cross-platform malware, and browser attacks
summary: JADEPUFFER, QuimaRAT, SkillCloak and a flaw in Opera GX are detected, while a Windows 11 code allows the capture of a hacker from the Scattered Spider group.

title: Computer memory stars handmade innovations and debates on minimum VRAM capacity
summary: An enthusiast builds a magnetic core memory amid high prices, Amazon showcases Graviton G5 bandwidth, and it is discussed whether 16 GB of VRAM will be enough for 1080p.

title: Linux renews itself with graphics support, new network tools, and a historic remaster
summary: The kernel fixes USB issues, Intel adds Xe3P support, DNSGlobe enables DNS monitoring, Organic Maps offers free maps, and Hannah Montana Linux reappears with updated Debian.

title: Bigme launches a 25.3-inch color e-ink monitor with 60 Hz refresh rate
summary: The device offers 3200x1800 resolution, 4096 colors, and a record refresh rate for E Ink, ideal for reading and work without eye strain.

title: The NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 12 GB is back in stock at Newegg for $329
summary: The MSI Ventus 2X OC graphics card is offered with free shipping, a 12 GB VRAM option at a competitive price.

title: A portable RTX 4080 GPU adapted to desktop offers performance similar to the RX 9070 GRE with lower consumption
summary: The device converts a laptop chip into a desktop graphics card that consumes only 100 W and costs 25% less.

title: The US Navy tests F/A-18 fighter parts made with 3D printing
summary: The composite components halve repair times and will be flight-tested on operational aircraft.

title: Intel prepares processors with up to 22 cores and motherboards with Panther Lake to compete with AMD
summary: Mini-ITX boards appear with the new 22-core Core Ultra 7 and 108 MB cache, while Nova Lake with Xe3P graphics are expected.

title: Lenovo bets on Chinese SSDs in its ThinkBook and launches a ThinkPad X1 Carbon with great battery life
summary: Faced with rising NAND memory prices, Lenovo installs YMTC drives in its laptops, while the ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 14 Aura Edition receives positive reviews.

title: News in handheld consoles: Ayaneo breaks price records, SteamOS improves, and tips for cloud gaming
summary: Ayaneo's new console costs almost four RTX 5080s, SteamOS fixes bugs on MSI Claw and Legion Go, and tips are offered for low-latency cloud gaming.

title: Space is the stage for ambitious projects: burning satellites, nuclear batteries, and a magnetic airbag
summary: SpaceX eliminates 260 satellites in the atmosphere, DARPA studies nuclear waste batteries for drones, and scientists propose a magnetic shield to protect Earth from solar storms.

title: Notable financial results: Samsung Foundry returns to profit, new unicorns, and Amazon closes Mechanical Turk
summary: Samsung's foundry posts profits for the first time in three years, 90 new unicorns are created, Amazon stops accepting new customers on Mechanical Turk, and Bending Spoons goes public.

title: The world's largest video game archive closes due to lack of funds as the community debates digital ownership
summary: The German project preserving 60,000 games is abandoned due to lack of funding, and the debate on the importance of owning physical versus digital games is reopened.

title: Jim Keller's startup, Fab2, will build a factory to produce small semiconductor plants
summary: Atomic Semi renames itself Fab2 and plans to revolutionize chip manufacturing with modular, low-cost factories.

title: A Delta flight is hit by a firework during landing at Midway Airport
summary: The incident occurred on July 4 with no serious injuries, but caused damage to the aircraft and prompted an investigation.

title: Fossilized excrement discovered in the Gobi Desert reveals a 4,000-year-old forest
summary: The finding of coprolites shows that the current most arid desert in Asia was a forest, providing data on past climate changes.

title: China invents a climate control system that cools buildings using rainwater without electricity
summary: The technology uses water evaporation to reduce building temperatures, decreasing energy consumption and emissions.
